rulings.Case Title: LEONARD MELLO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Consolidate Actions For All Purposes Including Trial
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion to consolidate actions numbered 274888 and 275916 for all purposes including trial pursuant to C.C.P § 1048 and 377.62 is granted. Both actions involve common questions of law or fact and defendants will not be prejudiced by consolidation. The new consolidated actions will maintain the August 16, 2012, trial setting conference date. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY MARZOLLA et al VS. AC AND S,INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Deft J And H Marine & Industrial Engineering Company'S Mo To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Sears, Roebuck and Co.¿s joinder in J and H Marine & Industrial Engineering Company¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420(a)(2)(B) is denied as untimely. The underlying motion went off calendar and defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to CRC 3.1342(a), which requires 45 days notice. The proof of service demonstrates that defendant electronically served plaintiffs with its joinder on May 8, 2012. The hearing date was set for June 20, 2012. Notice was improper. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE MAHER VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Complaint With Prejudice For Failure To Comply With Court Order To Amend, Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Strike Late-Filed Amended Pleading And For Dismissal With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Michelin North America Inc.¿s motion to dismiss plaintiff¿s complaint for failure to comply with court order to amend is denied. Defendant¿s alternative motion to strike for late-filed amendment pursuant CRC 31320 (i) is granted. Plaintiff failed to file its amended complaint within 10 days from the May 23, 2012 notice of entry of the court¿s order sustaining defendant¿s demurrer and never obtained leave from the court to file a late amendment. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
